note: My task only touches the NDCG class (compute method) in metrics.py to add graded relevance_scores support. If your feature also touches metrics.py, let's split by class/function region within the file so we don't collide, or take turns editing sequentially.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Fixing NDCG IDCG calculation to use len(expected_ids) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))

type: REVISE

your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Same file, same NDCG.compute method - real overlap. Proposal: sequential turn-taking instead of file split. My fix is a tiny one-line change (idcg ideal_length calc: use len(expected_ids) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))). I'll make that edit now and send DONE immediately (should take <1 min). Please wait for my DONE before editing metrics.py, then layer your relevance_scores change on top. OK?

I'll wait for your DONE, then apply my relevance_scores changes on top of your version. OK with going first?

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Done. Changed idcg calc to use range(1, len(expected_ids)+1) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)), and removed the now-unreachable 'idcg==0' early-return (expected_ids is guaranteed non-empty by the earlier validation, so idcg>0 always). No other lines in the file touched - safe for you to layer your relevance_scores changes on top now.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: Implemented relevance_scores support on NDCG.compute: added optional relevance_scores: Dict[str,float] param, a relevance(docid) helper (falls back to 1.0/0.0 binary behavior when not provided), used it for both DCG and IDCG (sorting ideal relevances descending for graded IDCG). Kept the existing min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) ideal_length and idcg==0 guard in my sandbox's base file since I only had visibility into the original code, not your applied diff (separate sandboxes - my working tree stayed clean/unmodified until my own edits). All 42 existing tests in test_metrics.py pass. My part is complete.
